mode1.top_risk: Without rate-limits and collusion resistance, karma collapses into a second activity/social-clique counter rather than a distinct appreciation signal.
mode1.cheapest_evidence: Ship a minimal grant command with a hard daily cap, log every grant with grantor/grantee, then measure grant-graph reciprocity and correlation with XP before building the leaderboard.

mode1.top_risk: Karma collapses into a second activity counter, gameable by mutual-boosting/alts, so it duplicates XP rather than adding a distinct signal.
mode1.cheapest_evidence: Ship a capped, log-only karma grant behind a flag and correlate karma vs XP rankings after a few weeks; low correlation + non-trivial grant volume validates distinctness.

mode1.top_risk: Karma collapses into a second activity counter (or a collusion/alt-farmed number), so it duplicates XP instead of measuring a distinct 'appreciation' axis.
mode1.cheapest_evidence: Run a time-boxed manual/thin-slice trial and correlate per-member karma against XP rank; low correlation plus a plausible top-N shows the axis is distinct and not trivially gamed.

mode2.top_risk: Karma collapses into a second activity counter (or a gameable popularity/collusion contest) rather than a distinct 'appreciation' signal.
mode2.cheapest_evidence: Run a 2-4 week shadow trial in one active server (grants logged, leaderboard hidden): rank-correlate karma vs XP and measure reciprocal-pair grant concentration; low correlation + healthy non-pair grant volume validates distinctness before any leaderboard ships.

mode2.top_risk: Karma collapses into a second activity counter - gameable by mutual-boosting, reciprocal cliques, and alts - duplicating XP instead of measuring a distinct appreciation axis.
mode2.cheapest_evidence: Config-flagged single-server pilot: log every grant, then measure karma-vs-XP rank correlation plus the share of karma flowing inside small reciprocal clusters - low correlation + low clustering is the load-bearing signal it's distinct.

mode2.top_risk: Karma collapses into a second activity counter - highly correlated with XP - so it earns its own leaderboard without carrying a distinct 'appreciation' signal, and mutual-boosting/alts launder social gaming as reputation.
mode2.cheapest_evidence: Ship a bare, non-spendable +karma command to one active server, log every grant for 2-4 weeks, then compute karma-vs-XP rank correlation (Spearman, thresholds fixed in advance) and inspect the directed grant graph for reciprocal pairs.

judges.why: Both grounded in actual mechanics with no fabrication and name correlation+reciprocity falsification; B edges ahead on the anti-gaming-defenses-worsen-redundancy trap, cold-start/thin-liquidity, moderation load, and shadow-log-before-visible insight.

Both nail distinctness-collapse with grounded non-fabricated mechanics; A edges ahead on the anti-gaming-defenses-worsen-redundancy trap, the shadow-trial insight that publishing creates the gaming it measures, and explicit staged decision rules.

instrumentation_note: Full usage metadata WAS visible from each Agent completion result via a <usage> block exposing subagent_tokens (integer), tool_uses (integer), and duration_ms (integer). All 14 sub-agents (3 mode-1, 9 mode-2 personas, 3 mode-2 synths, 2 judges) reported real values; none were null or invented. tokens = subagent_tokens; wall_ms = duration_ms. Mode-2 cell_tokens = sum of 3 persona tokens + synth tokens. Mode-2 cell_wall_parallel_ms = max(3 persona wall_ms) + synth wall_ms (personas ran in one parallel batch, synth sequentially after). Note: mode-2 rep-1 personas and rep-2/rep-3 personas were launched in two separate parallel waves, but each rep's 3 personas were genuinely concurrent, so max()+synth is the correct wall model per rep.
